maintenance: Station 85 (Zone 4) Main Unit 4 Exchange in progress — Job 124601, effective April 17, 2026; secondary and interruptible capacity impacts expected at this key Zone 4 compressor station

Station 35 (mainline MP 327.27) compressor outage April 7 — capacity restricted to ~80% of design; primary firm cuts of 15–20% reported per Notice 16654056; TSB incorporated into current constraint notices
Station 62-23 SELA tool runs (ILI inspection) completed April 1, 2026 — Job 1211791; SELA segment flows showing zero available secondary capacity in current constraint notices

Transco 2026 Major Construction and Maintenance Projects schedule updated March 12, 2026 (Notice 16650315) — some 2026 projects will affect IT and secondary FT services; ILI (inline inspection) activities section newly added; all dates subject to change

Southeast Supply Enhancement (SSE) — 1.6 Bcf/d expansion project relieving capacity constraints near Station 165 (VA) through Carolinas, Georgia, and Alabama — FERC-certified January 2026; construction targeted Q4 2026, in-service Q4 2027; $1.53 billion project fully subscribed

Northeast Supply Enhancement (NESE) — groundbreaking April 14–15, 2026 for NY/NJ/PA Transco expansion adding 400,000 Dth/d; first new pipe in New York state in over a decade per Williams CEO; in-service target Q4 2027
Force majeure Station 115 (unplanned equipment outage) — February 11–17, 2026; resolved
Force majeure Station 517 MU5 — December 14, 2025 through January 3, 2026; mainline flows limited to approximately 2,375 Mdth/d through that location during outage; resolved
No active force majeure or OFO notices found publicly for stations 65, 134, or 210 as of April 21, 2026
pricing: Transco Zone 6 NY spot: $2.43/MMBtu for April 20, 2026 delivery (+34.8% day-over-day) — EIA daily prices
Henry Hub spot: $2.81/MMBtu for April 20, 2026 (+3.8% day-over-day)
TETCO M3 (Mid-Atlantic, proxy for Zone 6 non-NY): $2.40/MMBtu (+25.5%)
Houston Ship Channel: $2.30/MMBtu (+3.7%); Henry Hub-to-Zone 6 NY spread approximately +$0.38/MMBtu on April 20
Transco Zone 3 (Station 65) and Zone 4 (Station 85) daily spot prices are paywalled at NGI/ICIS — not publicly accessible for April 21, 2026
Zone 4 basis futures traded on ICE (product 6590165: Transco Station 85 Zone 4 Basis Future); Zone 5 basis on ICE product 42944091
EIA April 2026 Short-Term Energy Outlook trimmed full-year 2026 Henry Hub forecast citing near-average storage trajectory
Southeast natural gas forwards gaining vs. Appalachia as demand diverges — data center power load growth increasingly driving spread volatility on Transco corridor per NGI April 2026
flow: Imbalance OFO active April 18–20 in Zones 5 and 6 (Notice 16765804): 7% or 1,000 dth/zone tolerance; minimum $50/dt/day penalty per Tariff Section 52; retro scheduling available; Transco cited 'limited flexibility due to shipper imbalance trends and current storage inventory levels'

Current Market and Production Constraints update (Notice 16780975, effective April 22 gas day): CS 90 (Zone 4) — 500 Mdt/d secondary S-to-N; TSB MP 890.58–1050 (Zone 4) — 500 Mdt/d secondary; CS 135 (Zone 5) — 500 Mdt/d secondary S-to-N; Marcus Hook Lateral (Zone 6) — 200 Mdt/d secondary; Trenton Woodbury Lateral (Zone 6) — 200 Mdt/d secondary
HBLV swing service effectively unavailable system-wide: 0 Mdt/d secondary in Zones 2–6 per current notice; Zone 1 shows only 1 Mdt/d — no meaningful swing buffer
SELA segment (Zones 3–4) showing zero available secondary capacity in current constraint notices for bidirectional flows
Central Penn Line northbound: approximately 1,112 Mdt/d primary available into Zone 6 per March baseline constraint notice
Recurring imbalance OFOs throughout April (April 1–6, April 9–13, April 16–20) — pattern indicates persistent system tightness entering spring shoulder season
EIA reports natural gas spot prices fell at most U.S. locations during week of April 16–23, 2026, consistent with spring demand moderation
Location-specific OFO risk: Transco issued Off-Rate Underperformance notice March 18 at LaSalle Tilden (Zone 1) — shippers warned volumes may be cut to actual flows if production does not recover

EIA storage report (week ending 04/10/26): working gas 1,970 Bcf, net injection +59 Bcf; 108 Bcf above 5-year average; 126 Bcf above year-ago; South Central region injected +32 Bcf (salt +17, nonsalt +15), Midwest +13, East +6
Projected EIA injection for week ending 04/17/26: ~94 Bcf per NGI consensus — would further expand surplus vs. 5-year norm
Storage entered injection season at approximately 1.9 Tcf (~3% above five-year average); EIA STEO projects storage to reach ~4.0 Tcf by end of October 2026, approximately 6% above seasonal normal
Dry gas production revised to 109.3 Bcf/d; LNG feedgas demand ~20 Bcf/d; Mexican pipeline exports ~6.5 Bcf/d — combined non-domestic demand providing floor support
NYMEX May 2026 contract: ~$2.61–$2.64/MMBtu as of mid-April; approximately $0.13 below prior week average; Henry Hub intraday 04/21/26 ~$2.69/MMBtu per TradingEconomics

Above-normal temperatures forecast across Gulf Coast and Southeast through late April 2026 — mild spring shoulder season suppressing heating and early cooling loads; Florida/Southeast demand estimated ~24.3 Bcf/d

Florida Gas Transmission (adjacent system to Transco Zone 3): scheduled maintenance at Compressor Stations 6 and 7 limiting capacity to 150 MMcf (CS6) and 300 MMcf (CS7); downstream Florida volumes have strengthened despite maintenance
